title: Scope fleet publication fingerprints to effective generator inputs
goal: Replace the over-broad RepoBrief fleet publication fingerprint with a versioned, explicit digest of effective generator/runtime inputs so test-, documentation- and timer-only Lenskit changes do not republish every repository while source, generator, contract and relevant configuration changes still invalidate safely.

acceptance.assertion: The fleet fingerprint is derived from a versioned, reviewable input contract that explicitly identifies the generator/runtime code, contracts and configuration capable of changing published bundle semantics.
Changing only tests, documentation, timer units or unrelated Lenskit modules leaves existing repository publication fingerprints unchanged.
Changing repository source SHA, effective generator code, output contracts or relevant publication configuration changes the fingerprint deterministically.
The fingerprint schema/version migration is explicit, fail-closed and auditable; malformed or unknown prior state is never silently treated as current.
Focused tests and a fleet dry-run prove zero unrelated republish candidates for a test/docs/timer-only change and expected candidates for a real generator change.
The optimization does not suppress source changes, bypass generator upgrades, weaken retention or reachability guards, or claim semantic equivalence without explicit inputs.

source_observation.interpretation: A test file under merger/lenskit changed the whole merger/lenskit tree digest and therefore invalidated every repository fingerprint, although no effective generator/runtime code changed.

metadata.blocked_reason: Implementation touches scripts/ops/rb-publish-fleet and its runtime tests, which remain under active foreign leases until the T026 retention work is terminal.
